input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition based on your project-naming convention. In `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Properties that apply to ALL projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Properties that apply ONLY to test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated early—before `PackageReference` items are resolved—so you can't condition on packages. The project name, however, is always available via the built-in `$(MSBuildProjectName)` property.

**If your naming varies** (e.g., `.Tests`, `.IntegrationTests`), broaden the condition:

```xml
Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.Contains('.Tests'))"
```

This keeps `MyApp.csproj` as a library and turns `MyApp.Tests.csproj` (and `MyApp.IntegrationTests.csproj`, etc.) into executables with the MTP runner enabled.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditionals. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of a test-related package reference or a naming convention.

The most robust approach for MSTest + Microsoft.Testing.Platform is to condition on the project file name or on whether a test SDK/package is referenced.

Common approaches:
1. Condition on project name convention (e.g., `*.Tests.csproj`)


2. Condition on whether `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or `MSTest` package is referenced
3. Use a custom property set in test projects

The key insight is that in `Directory.Build.props`, package references haven't been evaluated yet, so conditioning on `PackageReference` won't work. The most practical solution is to use a naming convention check like `Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))"` to target only test projects.
